channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Today’s Wordle #1810: Hints and Answer for June 3, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Wordle challenges players to guess a fresh five-letter word each day in six tries or fewer. Whether you’re a beginner or a daily player, today’s tips and strategies can help you solve it faster and maintain your streak—if you’re open to a little extra help. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Wordle, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answer for Wordle Game #1810 for June 3, 2026

To play today’s Wordle, head to the game’s website. If you want to go right to today’s answer without any tips or tricks, you can click right here.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s and yesterday’s editions of Wordle. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Wordle

Wordle is a simple but addictive word puzzle. Each day, players have to guess a five-letter word answer in no more than six attempts. After each guess:

  • Green letters are correct and in the right position.
  • Yellow letters are correct, but in the wrong position.
  • Gray letters are not in the word at all.

The goal is to guess the word in as few tries as possible. Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Wordle can only be played on the New York Times website. There is currently no app for you to download the official game.

Wordle Hints For Today, June 3, 2026

Today’s Wordle answer for game #1810 is a somewhat challenging puzzle. According to the NY Times Wordle review, today’s answer takes an average of 4.5 out of 6 guesses, which is slightly less than the 5-guess average of yesterday’s puzzle.

General Wordle Hints For June 3

Without giving today’s Wordle answer completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: It refers to a small cut, indentation, or groove in an object.
  • Hint 2: The word can also represent a level or step in a ranking system.
  • Hint 3: You might hear it in the phrase “take it up a ___”.
  • Hint 4: It can be found on tools, belts, wood, or other physical items where markings are useful

Use these clues to narrow down your guesses and increase your chances of solving Wordle #1810 for June 3, 2026.

Starter Words For June 3, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Wordle answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you figure out some included letters. A few starter words that may help are:

  • THORN
  • COUNT
  • NORTH
  • TONIC

Today’s Wordle Answer For Game #1810 On June 3, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answer to today’s Wordle below.

Answer: NOTCH

If you solved today’s Wordle without our answer, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

What Does Today’s Wordle Answer Mean?

NOTCH is a noun that means “an angular or V -shaped cut, indentation, or slit in an object, surface, or edge.” It can also mean “a step, degree, or grade.”

Yesterday’s Wordle Answer

If you missed the previous game, here’s yesterday’s Wordle answer for Game #1809 on June 2, 2026

Answer: BASIS

While not always the case, looking at previous words can sometimes give you insight into common patterns or letter usage in Wordle puzzles.

When Is The Next Wordle Game After #1810

Wordle games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours before to give players a heads-up. The game on June 4 will be Wordle Game #1811.

What Is Wordle?

Wordle is a daily word puzzle created by Josh Wardle. Initially developed as a prototype in 2013, it wasn’t fully released until 2021. The game challenges players to guess a five-letter word in six tries or fewer, using hints from each attempt to narrow down the answer. Its straightforward concept, accessibility, and the thrill of solving a new puzzle each day helped it surge in popularity by late 2021. The success of Wordle inspired numerous spin-offs and social media challenges, leading to its acquisition by The New York Times in early 2022.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Hell Let Loose Vietnam Delayed By Team17
item.content:encoded:
A new announcement from Team17 reveals that Hell Let Loose Vietnam has been delayed, with a new release date of August 13, 2026.

The developers recently held a free-to-play PC Steam open beta for Hell Let Loose Vietnam, during which many players experienced the game for the first time. There was a lot of feedback during this phase, and, along with internal testing, the developers decided that the best course of action was to delay the game for a better launch experience.

Hell Let Loose Vietnam’s New Release Date Set for August 13

Through a statement provided to Insider Gaming, Team17 has confirmed that Hell Let Loose Vietnam has been delayed to August 13. The developers stated that, along with the feedback received during the open and closed betas, Team17 conducted intensive internal playtests of the PC and console versions of the game, which led to the discovery of “several areas” that will require additional development time.

The developers have decided to delay the game’s release by approximately two months to “deliver a stronger launch experience.” They have apologized for the delay and promised to keep players updated on the development progress. The team is committed to achieving a level of quality that meets both its own expectations and those of the players. The original release date for Hell Let Loose Vietnam was scheduled for June 18, 2026, which was just over two weeks away.

In other news, Insider Gaming had the chance to get a hands-on experience of Hell Let Loose: Vietnam, and here’s how it went. What are your thoughts on Hell Let Loose Vietnam being delayed? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Marathon’s new experimental PvP-lite mode, Sponsored Survival. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Fnatic Bench Emypros and Hunt for Korean Toplaner, It’s Claimed
item.content:encoded:
Fnatic are set to bench Greek toplaner Panagiotis ‘Empyros’ Tantis from its LEC roster ahead of the Summer Split.

This is according to a report from Sheep Esports, who claim the organisation are now looking for a new toplaner.

Fnatic were eliminated from LEC Spring Playoffs qualification a month ago after losing 2-1 to Team Vitality.

In that series, rookie Empyros had a disastrous final game, falling three levels and 3k gold down to Kaan ‘Naak Nako’ Okan in the first 15 minutes.

Now it appears that his tenure with the team is over after just two splits in his first season in the top tier.

Fnatic Look to Korea to Boost Their LEC hopes

According to Sheep Esports, Fnatic are now looking to Korea’s second tier, LCK Challengers League (LCK CL), and have held talks with multiple Korean toplaners.

Fnatic already has another contracted toplaner in Spaniard Óscar ‘Oscarinin’ Muñoz Jiménez, who was benched at the start of the year and was unable to find a new team.

This means that Fnatic will potentially have three toplaners on its books, despite struggling financially after starting the year with the smallest coaching staff in the LEC.

The team also has former LEC toplaner Andrei ‘Odoamne’ Pascu, who joined Fnatic’s coaching staff midway through the season to try and help the team’s topside.

This will be the second year in a row that Fnatic has imported a Korean for the Summer Split, after signing now-NAVI midlaner Yoon ‘Poby’ Sung-won from T1 Esports Academy to take them to Worlds.

Fnatic have attended the last nine LoL World Championships, having not failed to qualify for the international tournament since 2016.

However, the organisation is currently likely to miss out, having failed to even make the LEC’s top six this split.

Will this change be enough to turn Fnatic into LoL Worlds contenders? Share your thoughts with us on the Insider Gaming Discord.

Meanwhile, the LEC top three is set after Karmine Corp eliminated GIANTX from the LEC Spring Playoffs.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: LoL Patch 26.12 Will Bring Huge Changes Ahead of MSI 2026
item.content:encoded:
Riot Games has released a preview for the next League of Legends update, Patch 26.12, which will be the last patch before the Mid-Season Invitational (MSI).

League of Legends MSI 2026 will be then be played on Patch 26.13, and it seems Riot is making significant changes before then with the large international esports tournament in mind.

The preview reveals that at least 15 champions will receive buffs or nerfs in the next patch, though these are subject to change as Riot is still collecting data from 26.11.

And there is also set to be an update to Teleport, which could have huge repercussions for pro-level games.

All of this is coming to the game, plus a huge ARAM Mayhem update and the introduction of Ranked 5s as revealed by the latest LoL dev update.

Changes Coming Expected in LoL Patch 26.12

  • Buffs
    • Yuumi
    • Jax
    • Gwen
    • Aatrox
    • Hwei
    • Syndra
    • Tristana
    • Sylas
  • Nerfs
    • xin Zhao
    • Nocturne
    • Lee Sin
    • Orianna
    • Ryze
    • Cassiopeia
    • Varus
  • System Adjustments
    • Teleport

Phroxzon Explains 26.12 Changes

Lead gameplay designer Matt ‘Phroxzon’ Leung-Harrison has broken down the proposed changes for the next patch.

Phroxzon revealed that the publishers aren’t too happy with instances in pro play where Teleport is used just for its shield:

“While this is a cool usage of the spell, it’s a pretty unintuitive behaviour, and we want to skew its usage to be better to use for its cross-map and joining fights capabilities,” the designer said.

As a result, Riot is looking to decrease the duration of the shield while making the shield a little bigger in compensation.

Making the shield bigger does sound counterintuitive, but it will depend on the final numbers.

Additionally, he revealed that Yuumi is getting compensation buffs for the Moonstone nerfs, and Lee Sin is being nerfed slightly after the changes to his kit in 26.10.

However, the main focus for this patch appears to be shaking up the mid lane meta as Phroxzon explained:

“For average play, AP Xin Zhao has been tearing up the rift with a lot of his power budget in healing, especially in the early lane creating some pretty uninteractive lane states.”

“Otherwise, [expect] a few adjustments to the champions who have a bit of room in Solo Queue, like Hwei, Sylas, Syndra and nerfs to some of the top-end like Cassio (who really benefited from Deathfire Touch being added), Ryze and Ori who have been strong for pretty much the whole season.”

What do you make of the proposed changes? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

Meanwhile, Riot has also revealed when the new T1 Worlds 2025 winners’ skins and next Hall of Fame skins will release.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/lol-patch-26-12-will-bring-huge-changes-ahead-of-msi-2026/

item.title: Marathon Devs Reveal ‘Sponsored Survival’, a PvP-Lite Mode
item.content:encoded:
Marathon’s Season 2 goes live today, and with it, players get a new PvP-lite-focused experimental mode, Sponsored Survival, with lower risk.

Players have been waiting for the new season for some time, especially since the developers announced that Marathon would receive two new experimental PvE modes in the new season. A new experimental mode is now already live and will remain accessible until June 9. Check out how it works below:

Marathon’s New Experimental Mode Starts as PvE, But Soon Lets Rooks In

Bungie has published a new blog post introducing players to the experimental Marathon game mode, Sponsored Survival. In this mode, players find themselves alone on the map, which is set in the new Night Marsh Zone. This allows them to explore and loot freely without the distraction of other players. However, it won’t be completely danger-free, as players will still encounter UESC, which can be quite challenging, along with new enemies to face later as well.

After a period of time, backfilled Rooks will enter the map. They can choose to team up with others or take on the map alone. The objective of this mode is to survive until the final exfiltration, with matches running on an 18-minute timer. Once the timer reaches zero, players must rush for the final exfil. However, things change at this point. During the exfil, players will have two options: they can either negotiate a temporary truce to exfiltrate together or turn their weapons on each other in the dead of night.

In other news, in a bid to save Destiny, fans have rallied behind a Destiny 3 petition, and it’s accrued more signatures than Marathon’s peak player count. Additionally, Marathon’s failure to deliver has had a massive impact on Sony and Bungie, with a $765 million impairment loss being recorded. What are your thoughts on Marathon’s Sponsored Survival experimental mode? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about ARC Raiders coming to Nintendo but not Switch 2. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: ARC Raiders Comes to Nintendo, But Not Switch 2
item.content:encoded:
ARC Raiders remains a sensational breakout extraction adventure title, even if sentiments have slipped a bit in the last couple of months. It maintains solid player retention and something of an aura, but some of Embark Studios’ latest changes have left players wanting something a little more meaningful.

On social media, images have cropped up showing how far some ARC Raiders players will go to drive the innovation of this game further, bringing their own meaningful changes into the picture. What follows is the tale of how one fan managed to get ARC Raiders working on a Nintendo 3DS XL.

ARC Raiders, But on Nintendo, But Retro

ARC Raiders is currently not available on Nintendo Switch 2, and there’s no indication that it ever will be. While Embark Studios is slowing down major updates and preparing for a Chinese version to hit the market, fans have taken it upon themselves to push the limits of the game as far as possible.

On Reddit, one user shared images of ARC Raiders playing on a 3DS XL console, and by all accounts, it works just fine.

But there’s a major twist, and that’s that the game isn’t running natively on the classic handheld device.

The user, YeezysKanye2020, was using an application called Moonlight to stream ARC Raiders from his desktop PC, powered by an RTX 5070 GPU, to the 3DS XL. Moonlight’s self-written description professes how the app can ‘allow you to play your PC games on almost any device,’ and that certainly rings true in this situation.

The unidentified user even managed to pair a Steam Controller to get away from the 3DS XL’s fiddly touch controls, just to make life easier. He stressed that ‘FPS isn’t actually the problem’, but the controls were the biggest blocker to extraction-based success on Nintendo’s 14-year-old handheld console.

Unfortunately, things turned a little awry (and I ceased advising to pursue replication) when YeezysKanye2020 admitted that a hacked 3DS XL is also needed to run ARC Raiders.

But still, the innovation of some gamers knows no bounds.

Do you wish ARC Raiders would come to Nintendo officially? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Fallout 76 current-gen versions are being tested

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP
